title: Solve similar triangles (basic)
description: Given two similar triangles and some of their side lengths, find a missing side length.

assessment_items.question_content:
Triangle $XYZ$ is similar to triangle $PQR$.

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/ee984a9b462b0c1adb41e9ca0b232cb795e6c8d4) ![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/3033c8fe68c94cfdef115a121ad1d4ee59fab6b4)

 **Solve for $n$.**

 $n=$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints: In similar triangles, the lengths of each pair of corresponding sides form an equivalent ratio.

Based on the similarity statement,

- $\blueE{\overline{XY}}$ corresponds to $\redE{\overline{PQ}}$
- $\blueE{\overline{YZ}}$ corresponds to $\redE{\overline{QR}}$
- $\blueE{\overline{ZX}}$ corresponds to $\redE{\overline{RP}}$

So, 

$\dfrac{\blueE{XY}}{\redE{PQ}} = \dfrac{\blueE{YZ}}{\redE{QR}} =\dfrac{\blueE{ZX}}{\redE{RP}}$

Let's fill in the given information.

$\dfrac{\blueE{35}}{\redE{28}} = \dfrac{\blueE{?}}{\redE{12}} =\dfrac{\blueE{30}}{\redE{n}}$

Since one part of the equation has no unknowns, we will use that part to solve for $n$.

$\begin{align}
\dfrac{\blueE{35}}{\redE{28}} &=\dfrac{\blueE{30}}{\redE{n}} \\\\
\dfrac{\blueE{5}}{\redE{4}} &=\dfrac{\blueE{30}}{\redE{n}} \\\\
(\redE{4n})\dfrac{\blueE{5}}{\redE{4}} &=(\redE{4n})\dfrac{\blueE{30}}{\redE{n}} \\\\
5n &=4(30)\\\\
n&=24
\end{align}$

So, $n=24$.

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/bc2091dfc9bcf3274053b08e9f8b40902e4d893b)

assessment_items.question_content:
Triangle $PQR$ is similar to triangle $FGH$.

 **Solve for $n$.**

 $n=$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]



[[☃ image 1]]
hints: Similar triangles have proportional sides.
Therefore, we can set up equivalent proportions and solve for $n$.

$\dfrac{\red{n}}{\blue{9}} = \dfrac{\red{4}}{\blue{6}}$

 Note: As each corresponding $\dfrac{\red{\text{side}}}{\blue{\text{side}}}$ proportion is equivalent, you could use the other sides (i.e., $\dfrac{\red{n}}{\blue{9}} = \dfrac{\red{7}}{\blue{10.5}}$)

Reduce the proportion on the right hand side.

 $\dfrac{\red{n}}{\blue{9}} = \cancel{\dfrac{\red{4}}{\blue{6}}}{\green{\dfrac{2}{3}}}$

Multiply each side by $9$ and simplify.

 $\cancel{9} \times \dfrac{\red{n}}{\cancel{\blue{9}}} = \green{\dfrac{2}{3}} \times 9$
$\red{n}$ is equal to $6$.

options.alt: Two triangles F G H and P Q R. Side F G is seven. Side H I is four. Side G H is n. Side P Q is ten point five. Side Q R is nine. Side R P is six.

assessment_items.question_content:
Triangle $FGH$ is similar to triangle $DEF$.

 **Solve for $p$.**

 $p=$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]



[[☃ image 1]]
hints: Similar triangles have proportional sides.
Therefore, we can set up equivalent proportions and solve for $p$.

$\dfrac{\red{p}}{\blue{12}} = \dfrac{\red{32}}{\blue{24}}$

 Note: As each corresponding $\dfrac{\red{\text{side}}}{\blue{\text{side}}}$ proportion is equivalent, you could use the other sides (i.e., $\dfrac{\red{p}}{\blue{12}} = \dfrac{\red{22}}{\blue{16.5}}$)

Reduce the proportion on the right hand side.

 $\dfrac{\red{p}}{\blue{12}} = \cancel{\dfrac{\red{32}}{\blue{24}}}{\green{\dfrac{4}{3}}}$

Multiply each side by $12$ and simplify.

 $\cancel{12} \times \dfrac{\red{p}}{\cancel{\blue{12}}} = \green{\dfrac{4}{3}} \times 12$
$\red{p}$ is equal to $16$.

options.alt: Two triangles F G H and D E F. Side F G is twenty-four. Side F H is twelve. Side G H is sixteen point five. Side D E is thirty-two. Side E F is twenty-two. Side D F is p.

assessment_items.question_content:
Triangle $ABC$ is similar to triangle $JKL$.

 **Solve for $q$.**

 $q=$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]



[[☃ image 1]]
hints: Similar triangles have proportional sides.
Therefore, we can set up equivalent proportions and solve for $q$.

$\dfrac{\red{q}}{\blue{24}} = \dfrac{\red{14}}{\blue{28}}$

 Note: As each corresponding $\dfrac{\red{\text{side}}}{\blue{\text{side}}}$ proportion is equivalent, you could use the other sides (i.e., $\dfrac{\red{q}}{\blue{24}} = \dfrac{\red{5}}{\blue{10}}$)

Reduce the proportion on the right hand side.

 $\dfrac{\red{q}}{\blue{24}} = \cancel{\dfrac{\red{14}}{\blue{28}}}{\green{\dfrac{1}{2}}}$

Multiply each side by $24$ and simplify.

 $\cancel{24} \times \dfrac{\red{q}}{\cancel{\blue{24}}} = \green{\dfrac{1}{2}} \times 24$
$\red{q}$ is equal to $12$.

options.alt: Two triangles A B C and J K L. Side A C is twenty-four. Side A B is twenty-eight. Side B C is ten. Side J K is fourteen. Side L K is five. Side L J is q.

[[☃ image 1]]
hints: Similar triangles have proportional sides.
Therefore, we can set up equivalent proportions and solve for $x$.

$\dfrac{\red{x}}{\blue{6}} = \dfrac{\red{18}}{\blue{9}}$

 Note: As each corresponding $\dfrac{\red{\text{side}}}{\blue{\text{side}}}$ proportion is equivalent, you could use the other sides (i.e., $\dfrac{\red{x}}{\blue{6}} = \dfrac{\red{14}}{\blue{7}}$)

Reduce the proportion on the right hand side.

 $\dfrac{\red{x}}{\blue{6}} = \cancel{\dfrac{\red{18}}{\blue{9}}}{\green{2}}$

Multiply each side by $6$ and simplify.

 $\cancel{6} \times \dfrac{\red{x}}{\cancel{\blue{6}}} = \green{2} \times 6$
$\red{x}$ is equal to $12$.

options.alt: Two triangles A B C and F G H. Side A C is x. Side A B is eighteen. Side B C is fourteen. Side F G is nine. Side G H is seven. Side H F is six.

 $k=$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]



[[☃ image 1]]
hints: Similar triangles have proportional sides.
Therefore, we can set up equivalent proportions and solve for $k$.

$\dfrac{\red{k}}{\blue{10.5}} = \dfrac{\red{5.5}}{\blue{16.5}}$

 Note: As each corresponding $\dfrac{\red{\text{side}}}{\blue{\text{side}}}$ proportion is equivalent, you could use the other sides (i.e., $\dfrac{\red{k}}{\blue{10.5}} = \dfrac{\red{6.5}}{\blue{19.5}}$)

 $\dfrac{\red{k}}{\blue{10.5}} = \cancel{\dfrac{\red{5.5}}{\blue{16.5}}}{\green{\dfrac{1}{3}}}$

Multiply each side by $10.5$ and simplify.

 $\cancel{10.5} \times \dfrac{\red{k}}{\cancel{\blue{10.5}}} = \green{\dfrac{1}{3}} \times 10.5$
$\red{k}$ is equal to $3.5$.

options.alt: Two triangles P Q R and X Y Z. Side P Q is six point five. Side Q R is k. Side R P is five point five. Side X Y is nineteen point five. Side Y Z is ten point five. Side Z X is sixteen point five.

Triangle $JKL$ is similar to triangle $PQR$.

 **Solve for $t$.**

 $t=$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]



[[☃ image 1]]
hints: Similar triangles have proportional sides.
Therefore, we can set up equivalent proportions and solve for $t$.

$\dfrac{\red{t}}{\blue{1.5}} = \dfrac{\red{22.5}}{\blue{4.5}}$

 Note: As each corresponding $\dfrac{\red{\text{side}}}{\blue{\text{side}}}$ proportion is equivalent, you could use the other sides (i.e., $\dfrac{\red{t}}{\blue{1.5}} = \dfrac{\red{25}}{\blue{5}}$)

Reduce the proportion on the right hand side.

 $\dfrac{\red{t}}{\blue{1.5}} = \cancel{\dfrac{\red{22.5}}{\blue{4.5}}}{\green{5}}$

Multiply each side by $1.5$ and simplify.

 $\cancel{1.5} \times \dfrac{\red{t}}{\cancel{\blue{1.5}}} = \green{5} \times 1.5$
$\red{t}$ is equal to $7.5$.

options.alt: Two triangles P Q R and J K L. Side Q R is t. Side P Q is twenty-five. Side P R is twenty-two point five. Side J K is five. Side J L is four point five. Side K L is one point five.

assessment_items.question_content:
Triangle $ABC$ is similar to triangle $XYZ$.

 **Solve for $t$.**

 $t=$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]

 ![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/bde009fa86b0e01504c02a42c0562f960cd37aec)
hints: Similar triangles have proportional sides.
Therefore, we can set up equivalent proportions and solve for $t$.

$\dfrac{\red{t}}{\blue{19.5}} = \dfrac{\red{4}}{\blue{12}}$

 Note: As each corresponding $\dfrac{\red{\text{side}}}{\blue{\text{side}}}$ proportion is equivalent, you could use the other sides (i.e., $\dfrac{\red{t}}{\blue{19.5}} = \dfrac{\red{4.5}}{\blue{13.5}}$)

Reduce the proportion on the right hand side.

 $\dfrac{\red{t}}{\blue{19.5}} = \cancel{\dfrac{\red{4}}{\blue{12}}}{\green{\dfrac{1}{3}}}$

Multiply each side by $19.5$ and simplify.

 $\cancel{19.5} \times \dfrac{\red{t}}{\cancel{\blue{19.5}}} = \green{\dfrac{1}{3}} \times 19.5$
$\red{t}$ is equal to $6.5$.

assessment_items.question_content:
Triangle $ABC$ is similar to triangle $PQR$.

 **Solve for $n$.**

 $n=$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]



[[☃ image 1]]
hints: Similar triangles have proportional sides.
Therefore, we can set up equivalent proportions and solve for $n$.

$\dfrac{\red{n}}{\blue{2.5}} = \dfrac{\red{16.5}}{\blue{5.5}}$

 Note: As each corresponding $\dfrac{\red{\text{side}}}{\blue{\text{side}}}$ proportion is equivalent, you could use the other sides (i.e., $\dfrac{\red{n}}{\blue{2.5}} = \dfrac{\red{15}}{\blue{5}}$)

Reduce the proportion on the right hand side.

 $\dfrac{\red{n}}{\blue{2.5}} = \cancel{\dfrac{\red{16.5}}{\blue{5.5}}}{\green{3}}$

Multiply each side by $2.5$ and simplify.

 $\cancel{2.5} \times \dfrac{\red{n}}{\cancel{\blue{2.5}}} = \green{3} \times 2.5$
$\red{n}$ is equal to $7.5$.

options.alt: Two triangles A B C and P Q R. Side A B is five point five. Side B C is five. Side C A is two point five. Side P Q is sixteen point five. Side Q R is fifteen. Side P R is n.

 $\dfrac{\red{y}}{\blue{37.5}} = \cancel{\dfrac{\red{16.5}}{\blue{27.5}}}{\green{\dfrac{3}{5}}}$

 $\cancel{37.5} \times \dfrac{\red{y}}{\cancel{\blue{37.5}}} = \green{\dfrac{3}{5}} \times 37.5$
